title: Join external FastF1 race-control and weather context by race-year-lap
summary: Your current best features are mostly single-row tyre-state heuristics.
rationale: Your current best features are mostly single-row tyre-state heuristics. FastF1 exposes lap timing plus track status, safety car/VSC, race control, and weather; F1 strategy articles explicitly note Safety Car windows trigger pit decisions. Those signals should explain many abrupt pit calls that tyre-life proxies miss.
implementation_hint: Build a left join on `Year` + `Race` + `LapNumber` (and driver where needed) using FastF1 race sessions. Add lap-level flags for safety car/VSC/red/yellow, recent status change, rain/wet track proxies, session messages, and possibly race-lap total pit counts. Keep it causal: only same-lap-or-earlier external info.
expected_effect: Should improve recall on sudden strategy pivots and wet/safety-car regimes; likely helps LB more than local CV if current folds underrepresent those regimes.
risk: Race-name alignment and lap indexing can be messy; external data could introduce silent join errors or leakage if post-lap messages are attached too early.
